_meta.note: Illustrative FitReport from POST /api/fit. Scores vary run to run — regenerate with `npm run bench -- --save`.
_meta.jobDescription: Forward-deployed / solutions engineer at an AI-native startup. React + TypeScript, comfortable talking directly to customers, ships fast, works across the stack, and translates messy user needs into working software.

tech_stack.rationale: React + TypeScript is his daily stack, and this very app is React 19 in strict mode.

experience_level.rationale: A 2025 grad with real shipped products, but fewer years than a mid-level hire would bring.

seniority.rationale: Operated as sole developer on a platform migration, though without formal senior tenure.

domain_fit.rationale: The role's build-plus-talk-to-customers shape matches his trainer-turned-developer background almost exactly.

working_style.rationale: Ships fast, thrives in ambiguity, and defaults to AI tooling — the described startup mode is his native one.

gaps: Early-career: less production tenure than a senior forward-deployed hire.
No named enterprise-scale deployment experience in the described domain.
tailored_pitch: Adam already lives the exact loop this role asks for — he built and shipped BodyCraft solo on a React/TypeScript-adjacent stack while at Sigo Signs he translated non-technical users' needs into a migrated TypeScript platform. Point him at your customers and he'll ship the software and run the conversation, which is precisely the forward-deployed profile you're hiring for.
